name: Crowd Energy
description: The spectators are lined up three deep, screaming your name like you're winning the Olympics. You're not. But it feels like it.

name: Runner's High
description: Everything clicks. Your breathing is easy, your legs feel light, the world is beautiful. This is why you run.

name: Second Wind
description: You were hurting. Now you're not. The legs have come back to life and the pace feels easy again.

name: Perfect Weather
description: Cool breeze, overcast skies, low humidity. Runners call this 'PR weather.' The conditions are doing half the work for you.

name: Downhill Cruise
description: A long, gradual descent. Gravity is your friend. Let it carry you.

name: Stomach Trouble
description: Your stomach is staging a rebellion. Something you ate is fighting its way back up. Every runner knows this feeling.

choices.label: Slow down and sip water
choices.outcome: The nausea passes after a few minutes. Smart call.

choices.label: Push through it
choices.outcome: Bad call. It gets worse before it gets better. Way worse.

choices.label: Walk for a bit
choices.outcome: Walking helps settle things. You lose time but feel human again.

name: Hot Spot
description: You feel that telltale warmth on the ball of your foot. Right now it's a warning. In two miles it'll be a blister.

choices.label: Stop and adjust (30 seconds)
choices.outcome: Quick sock adjustment. Crisis averted. Thirty seconds well spent.

choices.label: Ignore it
choices.outcome: You'll deal with it later. That's what every runner says right before they can't walk.

name: Side Stitch
description: A sharp, stabbing pain just below your ribs. The classic runner's side stitch. Breathing makes it worse.

choices.label: Slow down and breathe deeply
choices.outcome: Deep belly breaths. In through the nose, out through the mouth. It fades.

choices.label: Press on it and keep running
choices.outcome: The pressure helps a little, but you're grimacing through every step.

name: Headwind
description: The wind shifts and you're running straight into it. Every step feels like pushing through a wall of air.

name: Wrong Turn
description: You missed a course marker. By the time you realize, you've added distance. A volunteer waves you back on course.

name: The Wall
description: Mile 20. Your glycogen is gone. Your legs are concrete. Every cell in your body is begging you to stop. This is the marathon.

choices.label: Dig deep
choices.outcome: You find something inside you didn't know was there. This is what you trained for.

choices.label: Walk and regroup
choices.outcome: Walking lets you gather yourself. Many marathoners walk the wall. No shame in it.

name: Muscle Cramp
description: Your calf seizes up mid-stride. The kind of cramp that stops you dead and makes you grab the muscle like it owes you money.

choices.label: Stop and stretch it out
choices.outcome: Thirty seconds of stretching on the side of the road. It releases. Keep moving.

choices.label: Run through it
choices.outcome: It loosens eventually, but every step until then is agony.

choices.label: Walk until it passes
choices.outcome: Walking gives the muscle time to relax. Slow but safe.

name: Dehydration Warning
description: Your mouth is dry, your head is pounding, and the world is getting a little fuzzy around the edges. You need fluids. Now.

choices.label: Drink everything at the next aid station
choices.outcome: Water, electrolytes, more water. You feel the fog lift after a few minutes.

choices.label: Ration what you have and push on
choices.outcome: Small sips. It's not enough. The headache stays.

name: Fellow Runner Struggling
description: A runner beside you is in rough shape. Hands on knees, breathing hard, eyes unfocused. They look like they might drop.

choices.label: Run with them and offer encouragement
choices.outcome: You match their pace and talk them through it. They look up and nod. 'Thank you.' This is what running is about.

choices.label: Point them to the next aid station
choices.outcome: A quick word and a pointed finger. It's something. You hope they make it.

choices.label: Keep running
choices.outcome: You run past. You're racing too. But you think about them for the next mile.

name: Porta-Potty Emergency
description: Nature calls. Urgently. You spot the row of blue boxes ahead. There might be a line.

choices.label: Stop and wait in line
choices.outcome: Sweet relief. Three minutes you'll never get back, but you can focus on running now.

choices.label: Hold it and keep going
choices.outcome: Every step is a negotiation with your body. This is not the suffering you signed up for.

name: Spectator Sign
description: A hand-painted sign in the crowd catches your eye: 'Worst Parade Ever.' You laugh out loud for the first time in miles.

name: Shoelace Untied
description: You feel your left shoe getting loose. The lace is flapping. Every runner's minor nightmare.

choices.label: Stop and retie
choices.outcome: Double knot this time. Lesson learned.

choices.label: Keep running and hope for the best
choices.outcome: The loose shoe shifts with every step. Your foot is not happy about this decision.
